title: The AI skills that will be valuable in 2025 vs. 2024
post:
The AI skills that were valuable in 2024 are table stakes now. Prompting basics? Everyone knows those. Basic ChatGPT usage? That's expected.

What will actually be valuable in 2025: knowing how to evaluate AI outputs critically, understanding when to trust AI vs. when to override it, and — this is the big one — being able to architect workflows that combine human judgment with AI execution. The people who will thrive aren't the ones who use AI most. They're the ones who use it most strategically.

Are you building skills for where AI is going, or where it was? Thoughts?
